interface_specification_complete.notes: Interface contracts defined in module-design.md: 4 cross-module contracts (DEBATE_CONTEXT, Critique Report, Updated Artifacts, debate-summary.md). This is a prompt-engineering project; 'interfaces' are prompt-level contracts between agents, not APIs.

all_modules_designed.notes: 5 modules designed (M1-M5) with exact section structures, input/output contracts, and validation rules. M1: Orchestrator debate loop generalization. M2: Architecture Critic agent. M3: Architecture Refiner agent. M4: Solution-Architect Creator awareness. M5: isdlc.md command updates.

ui_wireframes_exist.notes: This is a CLI/prompt-engineering project with no UI. No wireframes are applicable. Agents are .md files with no visual interface.
ui_wireframes_exist.justification: Project type: prompt-engineering (markdown agents, no frontend)

user_flows_documented.notes: Data flow diagrams document all user-facing and inter-agent flows: generalized debate loop flow, Phase 03 concrete instance (sequence diagram), data transformation map, state management flow, 3 edge case flows.

error_taxonomy_complete.notes: 6 error categories defined: Debate Resolution (3 codes), Creator (3 codes), Critic (4 codes), Refiner (4 codes), Convergence (2 codes), State (3 codes). Total: 19 error codes. 4 error response patterns documented.

validation_rules_defined.notes: 62 validation rules across 5 modules. Each rule specifies: target file, string pattern, assertion type (contains/not_contains/contains_all/contains_any), and requirement traceability. Covers all 7 FRs and 30 ACs.

designs_cover_all_requirements.notes: Traceability verified: FR-001 -> M2 (20 rules), FR-002 -> M3 (16 rules), FR-003 -> M1+M5 (20+4 rules), FR-004 -> M4 (6 rules), FR-005 -> M1 (routing table rules), FR-006 -> M2+M1 (artifact naming), FR-007 -> M1+error-taxonomy (edge cases). All 4 NFRs addressed.

coverage.FR-006: M2 output format (ADR Count, Threat Coverage, NFR Alignment Score)
coverage.FR-007: M1 edge cases + error-taxonomy.md (19 error codes)
coverage.NFR-001: Sequential delegation (no parallel overhead) documented in data flow
coverage.NFR-002: Structural consistency validated by M2-V20, M3-V16, M2-V01, M3-V01
coverage.NFR-004: Constitutional compliance: M2-V19 (Article III/IV/V in critic)

interface_contracts_reviewed.notes: 4 interface contracts defined and verified against prior art (REQ-0014 Phase 01 agents). Contract 1 (DEBATE_CONTEXT) matches Phase 01 format. Contract 2 (Critique Report) extends Phase 01 with architecture metrics. Contract 3 (Updated Artifacts) same pattern. Contract 4 (debate-summary.md) extends with architecture metrics.

Article_I.notes: Designs implement architecture specifications exactly. Module designs follow ADR-0001 (routing table), ADR-0002 (phase-keyed lookup), ADR-0003 (convergence reuse), ADR-0004 (phase-agnostic naming). All 30 ACs mapped to specific validation rules.

Article_IV.notes: All design decisions documented explicitly. No undocumented assumptions. Ambiguities marked with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] pattern in M3 escalation process. Interface contracts fully specified.

Article_V.notes: Simplest solution: routing table in existing orchestrator section (not per-phase duplication). No new files beyond what architecture specified. No over-engineered abstractions.

Article_VII.notes: Every module traces to specific FRs and ACs. Traceability summary shows 7/7 FRs, 30/30 ACs covered. Validation rules include traces field mapping to requirement IDs.

Article_IX.notes: All required design artifacts present: module-design.md (interface specs + module designs), data-flow-design.md (user flows + data flows), error-taxonomy.md, validation-rules.json. Gate checklist complete.

deviations.justification: This is a prompt-engineering project with no visual UI. Agents are markdown files. No wireframes applicable.
This is a prompt-engineering project with no HTTP APIs. Interface contracts are prompt-level (DEBATE_CONTEXT blocks, critique report schemas) documented in module-design.md.

artifacts_produced.description: Comprehensive module designs for M1-M5 with interface contracts, validation rules, and traceability
Data flow diagrams (5 Mermaid diagrams), data transformation maps, state management flow, edge case flows
Error taxonomy with 19 error codes across 6 categories, 4 error response patterns, severity definitions
62 validation rules across 5 modules with assertion types and requirement traceability

key_inputs: module-design.md -- defines exact section structures and validation rules for each module
validation-rules.json -- 62 machine-readable validation rules ready for test case derivation
error-taxonomy.md -- 19 error codes with expected handling behavior (test each error path)
data-flow-design.md -- data transformation maps for integration test scenarios
handoff_to_next_phase.test_approach: CJS files using node:test that read .md files and assert string content patterns per validation-rules.json
